note: Phenylalanine is an essential amino acid in humans (no biosynthetic pathway). All inborn errors here (PKU, BH4-deficient hyperphenylalaninemias) are defects in Phe catabolism (PAH or BH4-cofactor-dependent conversion of Phe to Tyr). Catabolic replacement is unambiguous.

Although humans biosynthesise tyrosine from phenylalanine, no known human disease results from defective tyrosine biosynthesis. All members (tyrosinemias I/II/III, alkaptonuria, hawkinsinuria, oculocutaneous albinism, DOPA-responsive dystonia) are defects in tyrosine catabolism/utilisation (FAH/TAT/HPD/HGD/tyrosinase/TH).

Tryptophan is an essential amino acid in humans (no biosynthetic pathway). Members (hydroxykynureninuria, familial hypertryptophanemia, Lys/OHLys/Trp disorder) are defects in Trp catabolism (kynurenine pathway). Catabolic replacement is unambiguous.

Valine is an essential branched-chain amino acid (no human biosynthesis). The natural members (MSUD, isovaleric acidemia, propionic/methylmalonic acidemia for the BCAA branch) are all defects in BCAA catabolism (BCKDH and downstream enzymes). Catabolic replacement is unambiguous.

mondo_name: inborn disorder of amino acid and other organic acid metabolism
note: Umbrella for inborn organic acidemias and aminoacidopathies, which are accumulation diseases caused by blocks in catabolic pathways. The biosynthetic alternative does not fit the clinical class. Catabolic replacement is the right default; if a rare biosynthetic-defect child is later added (e.g. some primary hyperoxaluria phenotypes), revisit.

Same reasoning as MONDO:0019189: organic acidurias/acidemias and aminoacidopathies are catabolic accumulation disorders. Catabolic replacement is the right default for the umbrella; revisit if biosynthetic-defect children are added.
